title: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Search Query Parameter

steps.message: Search logs for the specific payload '<script>alert(1)</script>' and variations to identify the scope of the attack and if other users were affected.

Apply the primary remedy: Update the application code to HTML-encode all user-controllable data in the 'q' parameter before it is reflected in the response. Ensure the application explicitly specifies an encoding type (e.g., charset=UTF-8) in the Content-Type header to prevent character set manipulation.
Configure WAF rules to block common XSS bypass strings identified in the KB, such as script pseudo-protocols, unusual character sets, and nested script tags (e.g., <scr<script>ipt>).

Review the implemented encoding fix and verify using the standard proof-of-concept string '><script>alert(document.cookie)</script>' to ensure the vulnerability is closed and cannot be bypassed via DOM-based or filter-circumvention techniques.
Notify executive leadership of the incident resolution and initiate a security audit of all reflecting parameters within the application to prevent similar first-order XSS vulnerabilities.

params.text: Incident PB-XSS-001 resolved. Reflected XSS in /search mitigated via HTML encoding.
